title: Çılbır (Turkish eggs)

overview.description: Turkish poached eggs served over garlicky yoghurt and finished with a warm butter and chilli sauce.

ingredients: 1 tsp Aleppo pepper (or ½ tsp paprika plus a pinch of chilli flakes)
method: Bring the yoghurt to room temperature.
Mix the yoghurt with the garlic and a pinch of salt.
Bring a saucepan of water to a gentle simmer.
Poach the eggs for 3–4 minutes until the whites are set and the yolks remain runny.
Meanwhile, melt the butter in a small pan.
Add the Aleppo pepper and mint.
Place the poached eggs on top of the yoghurt.
Spoon over the warm butter sauce.

learning.did-you-know: Çılbır dates back to the Ottoman Empire and has been enjoyed in Turkey for centuries.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How warm and cold elements can be combined to create contrast in a dish, and how flavoured butter can transform simple ingredients.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A simple but impressive egg dish built almost entirely from staples already found in the Chapman Kitchen.
chapman-tips: Cold yoghurt straight from the fridge can dull the flavours—room temperature works much better.
Don't let the butter brown unless you specifically want a nuttier flavour.
Good bread is essential for mopping up the yoghurt, egg yolk and butter.
Fresh eggs make poaching much easier.
Warm the serving bowls with hot water while poaching the eggs. The dish comes together very quickly and warm bowls stop the yoghurt and eggs cooling down too fast.
